articles.title: Iran will continue to defend itself against aggression, ambassador to UK says
articles.body: But in the wake of President Masoud Pezeshkian's apology, Iran's ambassador to the UK agreed to our request to speak to him, and even more unusually, invited us to speak to him in Iran's embassy in London, a building that tells the story of the fraught and troubled history between Iran and the West.

articles.title: Armed robots take to the battlefield in Ukraine war
articles.body: Russia has also been developing combat UGVs, such as the Kuryer. According to Russian media reports, it can be equipped with a flame-thrower, a heavy machine gun normally found on tanks, and it can run autonomously for five hours.

articles.title: We've been speaking to Iranians during one week of war. Here's what they said
articles.body: My colleague Ghoncheh Habibiazad, who has been collecting voices from inside the country, says the views of some people have changed as the war has continued, as they did not expect it to go on after Khamenei was killed.

articles.title: Nairobi floods: At least 23 dead after heavy rains with Kenyan military deployed
articles.body: "The torrential rains have led to significant flooding, unfortunately resulting in 23 fatalities so far, the destruction of property, road closures, and the displacement of residents," police said in a statement on Saturday afternoon.

articles.title: Israeli troops push into Lebanon to seize key border positions
articles.body: But just days into this latest conflict, the mood in Israel - basking in the military partnership with Donald Trump - is already breathlessly looking to a new future, and a new Middle East, prompting one Israeli commentator, Avi Issacharof, to urge his country to "come down to earth for a moment".

articles.title: Ngoshe attack: 'Massive' numbers killed, Nigerian Senator Ndume tells BBC
articles.body: "I can confirm that there was an unfortunate incident and, due to the efforts of security personnel, we were able to repel the insurgents," he said, adding that a search and rescue operation was under way.

articles.title: Swiss to vote on right-wing push to slash licence fee for public broadcaster
articles.body: He also worries about the impact on SBC's coverage of foreign news. The broadcaster maintains correspondents in the US, Russia, China, the Middle East and Latin America, as well as in Brussels, Berlin, Paris, Rome and London.

articles.title: Russian strike on Kharkiv apartment block kills ten
articles.body: Detailing the aftermath of the strikes, Synegubov said that in Kharkiv, a 65-year-old woman, a 40-year-old man, and a 13-year-old girl were among those killed.

articles.title: China spent years building ties in Latin America. Can Trump make room for the US?
articles.body: She will be joined by conservative leaders from eight nations — Argentina, Paraguay, El Salvador, Chile, Panama, Honduras, Guyana, and Ecuador — who share ideological ties with the Trump administration. Colombia, Mexico, and Brazil will be absent.

articles.title: Indonesia to ban social media and other online platforms for under 16s
articles.body: "This will start with platforms such as YouTube, TikTok, Facebook, Instagram, Threads, X, Bigo Live, and Roblox," said Hafid, adding that the ban would make Indonesia "the first non-Western country to delay children's access to digital spaces according to age".

articles.title: Ukrainian bank workers released after detention in Hungary
articles.body: "To be honest, I would not restore it. This is my position. I expressed it to European leaders... because this is Russian oil," he told a briefing. "Russians are killing Ukrainians and we have to give oil to Orbán, because he, the poor thing, cannot win the election without this oil."

articles.title: US and Venezuela agree to resume diplomatic ties after Maduro capture
articles.body: Along with having the largest proven oil reserves in the world, Venezuela is rich in gold, diamonds, critical minerals and rare earth minerals - including some of those used in mobile phones.

articles.title: US economy unexpectedly sheds 92,000 jobs in February
articles.body: Employment in the federal government also continued to drop, shrinking by 10,000 last month. Since hitting a peak in October 2024, federal government employment has dropped by 330,000, or 11%, the Labor Department said.

articles.title: Piper James: Canadian teen died from drowning after dingo attack in Australia
articles.body: In 2023, a woman jogging along a beach on K'Gari was attacked by dingoes, forcing her to run into the ocean. Two witnesses saw her, pulled her out of the water and put her in the back of their vehicle before driving her to safety, according to local reports at the time.

articles.title: IRIS Dena: The final voyage of the Iranian warship sunk by the US
articles.body: The attack, Singh says, appears to have involved a single Mark-48 torpedo, a heavyweight weapon carrying about 650 pounds of high explosive, capable of snapping a ship in two. Video footage suggests the submarine may have fired from 3-4km away, around 05:30 local time.

articles.title: AI-generated Iran war videos surge as creators use new tech to cash in
articles.body: Google's AI tools, including its video generator Veo, are on the growing list of popular AI platforms, like OpenAI's Sora model, Chinese AI app Seedance, and Grok which is built into X.

articles.title: US President Donald Trump sets his sights on crisis-hit Cuba after Iran action
articles.body: Standing in front of an awkward-looking Lionel Messi, Luis Suarez and the other stars of the Inter Miami soccer team on Thursday, Trump told the team's owner, Cuban-born magnate Jorge Mas, that they would soon be "celebrating what's going on in Cuba", adding that the Cuban authorities "want to make a deal. So badly you have no idea."

articles.title: Iran conflict and China: How it is unsettling Beijing and its ambitions
articles.body: But then, he adds: "Probably, along with everyone else, they would also be thinking, oh God, they really have gone into this with no plan at all. Right, we don't want to get dragged into this like we don't want to get dragged into anything else, but we also need to do something."

articles.title: Australia: Fatbikes are wreaking havoc in Sydney's wealthy beach suburbs
articles.body: Observers say it's because of a combination of good bike-riding weather, parents with deep pockets – the cheapest model of one of the most popular brands retails for a minimum AUD$3,000 (US$2,110, £1,579) - and a lack of reliable public transport.

articles.title: Ethiopia's Tigray crisis: Why young women took up arms to fight Abiy Ahmed's government
articles.body: She saw herself as a feminist, taking on traditional attitudes about women's participation in sport. The teenager was also an outstanding student, studying IT in Tigray's main city of Mekelle, and had a clear vision for her future.

articles.title: Lebanon: Beirut evacuees 'sleeping in tents and cars'
articles.body:
In Lebanon, some residents in the southern Beirut suburb of Dahieh have left their homes amid evacuation orders and ongoing air strikes by Israel.

BBC Arabic's Carine Torbey says people from the area, which a Hezbollah stronghold, have been sleeping in tents and cars.

More on this story.

articles.title: After Iran, will Trump turn his eyes to Cuba?
articles.body:
Donald Trump's attention is seemingly turning to another Latin American regime: Cuba.

In recent months, the president has hinted at a possible takeover and during a White House event with the Inter Miami CF soccer team, the US president said the island was "ready to fall".

The BBC’s Will Grant explores the President Trump's recent comments and if his administration is indeed planning to target Cuba next.

articles.title: Watch: How war in Iran may affect food and fuel prices
articles.body:
As the US and Israel continue strikes on Iran, and with retaliatory strikes hitting nearby Middle East states, key shipping routes are being disrupted. Oil and gas production in the region is also being affected.

The BBC's Nick Marsh examines how the war could cause a rise in living costs around the world.

articles.title: Watch: Obama, Biden and Clinton honour Jesse Jackson at funeral
articles.body:
Former US presidents Barack Obama, Joe Biden and Bill Clinton were among dozens of high-profile figures present at the Reverend Jesse Jackson's funeral service. The men remembered Jackson for his 'passion' and for paving the way for Obama's presidential run.

Former vice-president Kamala Harris also spoke at the service and noted the country's political climate.

Jackson, a lifelong civil rights activist and former presidential candidate, passed away on 17 February.

articles.title: Inside Venezuela's political transition after Maduro's ousting
articles.body:
Venezuela has entered a period of political transition following the ousting of former president Nicolás Maduro on 3 January 2026. The US and Venezuela have agreed to re-establish diplomatic and consular relations, with the two sides promising to make an effort to promote stability, support economic recovery and advanced political reconciliation.

However, Venezuelans inside the country are still protesting the return of Nicolás Maduro.

The BBC’s Ione Wells reports from inside Venezuela, speaking to recently freed opposition politicians about the country’s uncertain transition and relations with the US.
